note: LIVE-data manifest for the lean-6module-v1.1 freshness gate. as_of pins to the LAST close = 2026-07-02 = $426.01 (NASDAQ). This close was up +5.87% on the day (prev close $402.38) -- an oversold bounce off a fresh 52-wk low ($396.68 set ~2026-06-30) + pre-Q2-earnings positioning (Q2 reports 2026-07-16); NO hard company/operational catalyst on the day. Price is triple-sourced (Yahoo chart API + stockanalysis + Google Finance), all $426.01, 0% delta. verify_freshness.py re-fetches Yahoo (last close <= as_of => 426.01) independently and cross-checks these declared sources + the decision_card value. FILED data (10-Q Q1 2026 reported 2026-04-21) is NOT here -- it rides the dated-source claim_ledger discipline. NOTE: ISRG did a 3-for-1 split (Oct 2024); all per-share figures are post-split. The Yahoo adj-close 52wk high reads $592.85 vs stockanalysis/Google intraday high $603.88; ISRG pays no dividend so the gap is close-basis vs intraday-high, not an adjustment error -- 52wk_high below uses $603.88 (2-source), but the price is well inside either band so T1/T2 are unaffected.

live_fields.note: Last close 2026-07-02 = $426.01 (-29.5% off 52wk high $603.88; +7.4% off 52wk low $396.68). Near the low, not hugging it (>3% off) and not near the high -> no high/low-grab error. +5.87% on the day (prev close $402.38); day range ~$406.47-$426.02 (closed at day high).
stockanalysis reports $150.88B at the $426.01 close. Cross-check: 354.4M shares x $426.01 = $150.98B (0.07% delta vs stockanalysis's 354.16M share basis). Consistent.

stockanalysis + Google Finance agree $603.88 (intraday high, ~Jan 2026). Yahoo adj-close series reads $592.85 (close basis). ISRG pays no dividend -> the gap is intraday-high vs close, not an adjustment error. Price $426.01 is well inside either band.
stockanalysis + Google Finance agree $396.68 (set ~2026-06-30, two days before as_of). Yahoo adj-close series reads $397.68 (close basis) -- within $1.

10-Q cover count 354.4M (as of ~2026-04-15 filing / 2026-03-31 period); stockanalysis current shares 354.16M; diluted weighted-avg 359.8M (Q1). Use ~354.4M for the mcap identity. Cross-source spread <0.1%.

live_fields.value: FY2026 guidance given at Q1 2026 print (2026-04-21): da Vinci procedure growth 13.5%-15.5% (midpoint ~14.5%; raised from 13-15% in Jan 2026); non-GAAP gross margin 67.5%-68.5% of revenue, including ~100bps estimated tariff impact + higher input costs; non-GAAP opex growth 11%-14%; non-GAAP tax rate 22%-23%. Q1 2026 actuals: revenue $2,770.8M (+23%), procedures +17%, non-GAAP EPS $2.50 (+38%). Next earnings Q2 2026 on 2026-07-16.

No material new litigation identified as of as_of. Standard product-liability and IP matters ordinary for a medical-device leader; not thesis-material. Watch items (not disclosed losses): (a) voluntary Class I/II recalls on certain da Vinci instruments (operational noise, 2026); (b) the competitive/regulatory landscape -- Medtronic Hugo US urology FDA clearance (2025-12-03) and J&J Ottava De Novo submission (2026-01-07) are competitive events, not litigation. No debt, so no covenant/credit litigation risk.
